summary: Zero-runtime TypeScript types for the WebMCP core surface, schema inference, and Navigator augmentation.
Strict WebMCP runtime polyfill for document.modelContext in browsers that do not ship the API yet.
Full MCP-B runtime entry point that layers transport and extension APIs on top of the strict WebMCP surface.

skill.name: mcp-b-global
skill.description: Use when writing code that registers AI tools on a webpage using document.modelContext, registerTool, provideContext, or the WebMCP / MCP-B browser runtime. Covers initialization, transport configuration, cleanup, and the full BrowserMcpServer API.

summary: Browser-adapted MCP TypeScript SDK that provides BrowserMcpServer and supports dynamic tool registration.
note: Most apps should use @mcp-b/global instead. Reach for this package when you need direct control over BrowserMcpServer.

summary: Tab, iframe, extension, and user-script transport classes for moving MCP messages through browser boundaries.
Custom element that exposes tools, prompts, and resources from an iframe to its parent page.
note: The iframe content must already expose document.modelContext through @mcp-b/global or another compatible implementation.

skill.name: mcp-iframe
skill.description: Use when bridging MCP tools, prompts, or resources from an iframe to its parent page using the <mcp-iframe> custom element. Covers attributes, events, name prefixing, lifecycle, and programmatic registration.

summary: Strict-core React hook for registering document.modelContext tools without the broader MCP-B runtime layer.
note: usewebmcp requires document.modelContext at runtime. Pair it with native browser support, @mcp-b/webmcp-polyfill, or @mcp-b/global.

skill.name: usewebmcp
skill.description: Use when registering WebMCP tools from React components using the useWebMCP hook. Lightweight strict-core hook for document.modelContext tool registration without MCP-B extensions.

summary: Full React integration for MCP-B, including tool hooks, prompts, resources, sampling, elicitation, and client providers.
note: Provider and client hooks assume @mcp-b/global or another BrowserMcpServer-based runtime is present.

skill.name: react-webmcp
skill.description: Use when building React apps with full MCP-B features: useWebMCP hook, prompts, resources, sampling, elicitation, and McpClientProvider. Supports Zod v3 object maps for input/output schemas.

summary: Local relay server that connects browser-hosted WebMCP tools to desktop MCP clients over stdio and WebSocket.

skill.name: webmcp-local-relay
skill.description: Use when connecting browser-hosted WebMCP tools to desktop MCP clients like Claude Code, Claude Desktop, or Cursor via the local relay server. Covers CLI options, embed script, static management tools, security, and troubleshooting.

summary: Chrome DevTools Protocol bridge that lets MCP clients inspect browsers and discover WebMCP tools from live pages.
note: This package is a fork of ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp with WebMCP integration layered on top.

summary: Chrome Extension API tool classes for tabs, bookmarks, history, storage, scripting, and related agent workflows.
Token-efficient DOM extraction library for AI agents, selector generation, and structured page understanding.
Parser, validator, prompt builder, and patch utilities for the AgentSkills SKILL.md format.
